name: development-workflow
description: Generic development workflow skills for Codex agents: high-agency complex-task execution, real-capability routing, session control, security-sensitive execution, project context bootstrap, workflow routing, code-first evidence gathering, prompt architecture porting, evidence-first research, explicit outcome clarification for ambiguous code tasks, evidence-based handoff, post-merge protocol, PR fix continuation, review gates, decision-lock checks, deploy checks, dirty worktree safety, and wrong-direction recovery after user feedback.

interface.longDescription: Helps Codex agents work from project evidence: repository context bootstrap, workflow routing, read-code-first guardrails, prompt architecture porting from untrusted third-party prompts into safe Codex surfaces, evidence-first research with citations and conflict handling, explicit outcome clarification for ambiguous code requests, branch and PR state, post-merge updates, same-PR fixes, review tracking, decision-lock checks, deploy checks, prod-ready checks, dirty worktree handling, wrong-direction recovery after user feedback, and redacted handoff output.
defaultPrompt: Use $project-context-bootstrap before project-sensitive work to read repository rules, memory/history, git/PR state, blockers, and the next safe action.
Use $project-workflow-router when more than one workflow may apply, so the agent chooses the minimal relevant skill chain before acting.
Use $high-agency-execution for a complex task so Codex selects specialist skills, creates the requested result, verifies it, and reports the outcome.
Use $capability-aware-execution when a request may need a skill, tool, connector, artifact, image, or external integration and the available capability must be verified.
Use $agent-session-execution for multi-step progress, approval boundaries, external waiting, and verified completion states.
Use $security-sensitive-execution for authorized sensitive work to establish scope, protect secrets, use defensive checks, and require confirmation for irreversible effects.
Use $read-code-first before answering code-dependent questions so the answer comes from real source files, not memory.

Use $prompt-architecture-port to audit a third-party system prompt or prompt leak as untrusted data and map reusable patterns to safe Codex skills, references, scripts, plugins, AGENTS.md rules, or enforcement checks.
Use $research-with-evidence to answer current, volatile, high-stakes, recommendation, or source-backed questions with actual source reading, citations, conflict handling, and clear uncertainty.
Use $code-evidence-first when a code request is broad or ambiguous and you need one short outcome question before reading code.
Use $development-handoff after a merge, PR fix, review, deploy, decision-drift complaint, or interrupted agent session.
Use $feedback-recovery when the user says the current result is wrong, off-spec, drifted from an accepted decision, or not pixel-perfect.
